items.title: Trump at 80: "Harry Houdini" & A Cage Fight
items.description: President Trump turns 80 today, and the White House lawn has been transformed into a giant arena, complete with floodlights, cameras, giant Star-Spangled Banners and a 90-foot scaffold known as “The Claw,” as 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue hosts “Freedom 250”, a celebration featuring professional UFC cage fighting. The event combines three occasions: President Trump’s birthday, Flag Day, which marks the adoption of the U.S. flag in 1777, and the launch of celebrations leading up to the 250th anniversary of American independence. Professor Jeffrey Sonnenfeld has known President Trump for 25 years and has critiqued his leadership style in the best selling book Trump’s Ten Commandments. He is also Senior Associate Dean for Leadership Studies at the Yale School of Management and has advised five U.S. presidents. He's been speaking to France 24's Gavin Lee.

Technical-level talks are set to follow next week as efforts continue to secure a lasting peace in the region.

items.title: As the world heats up, cities work to cool down
items.description: Global warming trends mean more frequent, intense heat waves. Cities around the world are working to mitigate extreme heat and adapt urban life to climate change.

items.title: Another decline for industrial output and construction activity in April
items.description: Construction fell 4% and manufacturing 2.1% from March, while most industrial sectors recorded year-on-year declines. Leer más

items.title: Gaza residents find brief escape in World Cup opener
items.description: Palestinian Territories residents gathered in tents, cafes and makeshift community spaces on 11 June to watch the opening match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, seeking a brief escape from the hardships of war.

items.title: Fuel shock sparks electric bike boom in Kenya
items.description: Rising fuel prices linked to the conflict in the Middle East are accelerating Kenya’s shift to electric mobility. As petrol costs soar, thousands of riders are abandoning fuel-powered motorcycles for cheaper electric alternatives, driving record sales and reshaping urban transport.

items.title: News24 | Pressure mounts on UK’s Starmer as defence minister John Healey quits
items.description: British defence minister John Healey quit over a months-long dispute over military spending, accusing UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer of failing to commit the resources that are needed to keep the country safe from mounting threats.

items.title: Angry reaction to Adorni’s admission that he concealed half-million dollars
items.description: Cabinet chief's admission that he omitted US$500,000 from his public asset and wealth declarations sparks controversy and criticism. Leer más

items.title: Sources say women who fled Iran to be deport from US to Central African Republic
items.description: Sources said two Iranian women will be among over 20 migrants to be sent to the Central African Republic under a so-called third-country deportees deal.

items.title: Jonathan Tah's first World Cup and the long wait
items.description: The 2026 World Cup will be the Bayern Munich defender's first for Germany. This moment has been a long time coming.

items.title: Tigrayan forces preparing offensive against federal gov, Addis Ababa warns
items.description: Fears are mounting in Ethiopia over reports that forces in the northern Tigray region are preparing an offensive against the federal government in the coming days.

items.title: Twelve killed as skydiving plane goes down shortly after take-off
items.description: The private plane had just taken off and made a turn before it crashed and caught fire, authorities said.

items.title: June 12: Broken bottle on our forehead, bludgeon on the back; democracy, is this your face?, By Festus Adedayo
items.description: It was the year 2000 or 2001. We were seated almost in a circumference. The venue was the Presidential Villa in Aso Rock, Abuja. We awaited the arrival of President OIusegun Obasanjo. The event was the monthly Presidential Media Chat. It was a forum where the president took questions from select editors. This evening, the […] The post June 12: Broken bottle on our forehead, bludgeon on the back; democracy, is this your face?, By Festus Adedayo appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Pressure mounts on Milei's Cabinet chief Adorni after cash stash admission
items.description: Lawmakers from opposition Coalición Cívica party file criminal complaint against Adorni for alleged "false statements," while sectors allied to the government called on Milei to withdraw his support for the official. Leer más

items.title: Arruabarrena set to return to Boca Juniors after talks with Riquelme
items.description: Former coach says he is 'excited' by prospect of a second spell at La Bombonera, a decade after leading the club to a league-and-cup double. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Trump eyes Iran’s Kharg Island, but taking it could hurt US troops, warn analysts
items.description: US President Donald Trump said he wanted to take over Kharg Island, Iran’s oil infrastructure hub.

items.title: Why the economics make this the craziest World Cup ever
items.description: From trade wars to soaring ticket prices, the 2026 World Cup is unlike any before it. Faisal Islam explores what this tournament reveals about our changing global economy.

items.title: World Bank warns of slower growth across Latin America
items.description: Argentina’s economy to grow 3.6% over the 2026–28 period, “driven by exports, but constrained by restrictive domestic monetary and fiscal policies,” forecasts World Bank. Leer más

items.title: Trump hosts White House cage fights to mark 80th birthday
items.description: The US president is set to watch seven fights with an audience of 4,000 people inside a specially built arena on the White House lawn. Critics say the unprecendented spectacle is in poor taste amid the ongoing Iran war.

items.title: On Argentina’s side of the Andes, Beijing builds a city for 5,000 miners
items.description: Lundin Mining-BHP copper mining project in San Juan Province is moving ahead with unprecedented infrastructure work, but the construction of a modular complex in the Andes for thousands of workers has put national industries on alert. Leer más

items.title: British artist David Hockney dies at 88
items.description: Considered a superstar of pop art, the UK painter was one of the world's most influential artists during his lifetime.

items.title: Back to the roots: Zimbabwe’s traditional food revival gains momentum
items.description: From bubbling sorghum porridge to village-reared chicken and ox-head stew, traditional food restaurants are booming in Zimbabwe’s second city, Bulawayo. Driven by health-conscious consumers and nostalgia for homegrown flavours, indigenous cuisine is making a strong comeback.

items.title: News24 | Why is a 'very close' Iran-US deal taking so long?
items.description: President Donald Trump has repeatedly declared Iran and the US are on the verge of agreeing on a deal to end the Middle East war, but a litany of sticking points has delayed finalising an accord and will complicate its implementation, analysts say.

items.title: News24 | 117.8 million people displaced in 2025, first drop in a decade, says UN refugee agency
items.description: The number of forcibly displaced people dropped for the first time in a decade last year, as more opted to return home, despite often unsafe and unstable conditions, the UN said.

items.title: Watch: Four UFO videos newly declassified by US government
items.description: The eyewitness footage shows bright orbs in the sky over an undisclosed location in the northeastern US.

items.title: Author Chimamanda Adichie accuses hospital of stalling review into son's death
items.description: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie's 21-month old son died in a Lagos hospital, which she says is trying to stop an inquest into the circumstances of his death.

items.title: Dominant Mexico defeat South Africa 2-0 in World Cup opener
items.description: Dozens of protesters clash with police outside Estadio Azteca; Mexico see off South Africa in bad-tempered opener that saw three red cards. Leer más

items.title: Swiss police fire tear gas as anti-G7 protesters target UN buildings
items.description: Swiss police on Sunday fired tear gas at demonstrators protesting the upcoming Group of Seven summit in France after some protesters started targeting buildings linked to the United Nations in Geneva.

items.title: ‘Losing three years set us back 20’: Palestinian football’s future in peril
items.description: The suspended Palestinian league is squeezing a generation of footballers, with little certainty about the future.

items.title: Lebanese Army withdraws from southern village after Israeli troops advance nearby
items.description: The departure from the Army barracks in Kfar Tebnit came as the Israeli military issued an evacuation warning for about 20 locations, including the southern city of Nabatiyeh and nearby villages

items.title: UK and Japan agree £18bn investment deal
items.description: Japanese firms will spend billions on UK infrastructure and offshore wind, Downing Street says.

items.title: Six killed as helicopters collide in Brazil’s Rio de Janeiro
items.description: The helicopters crashed into the parking lot of an electric car dealership, igniting a fire that engulfed at least 20 vehicles

items.title: Trump warns Israel and Iran not to ‘blow it’ after new strikes threaten emerging ceasefire deal
items.description: Trump, who had said the deal could be signed on Sunday (June 14, 2026), has pressed Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to stop hitting Lebanon hard while a deal is near, but the prime minister has defied him

items.title: Dangote Refinery targets $1 Billion fundraising
items.description: Nigeria’s Dangote Petroleum Refinery is taking a major step toward becoming a publicly traded company. The refinery is seeking to raise around one billion dollars through a private share placement, in a deal that values the company at roughly 39 billion dollars.

items.title: Anthropic staff to meet White House officials next week, Axios reports
Beirut strikes: 'No point' in talks if US cannot uphold 'commitments'
items.description: Iranian chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said on Sunday there was "no point" in continuing peace talks with the United States after its ally Israel attacked Beirut's southern suburbs.

items.title: Norway braces for verdict in rape trial of crown princess's son Høiby
items.description: Marius Borg Høiby will appear via video link, almost three months after his trial came to an end on 40 charges, including four counts of rape.

items.title: Russian drone barrage hits Kyiv and Mykolaiv as Ukraine reports major fires
items.description: Russian drone strikes hit multiple locations across Ukraine overnight into 12 June, damaging sites near Kyiv and in the Mykolaiv region, injuring one person and triggering a major fire, while Ukrainian air defences said they intercepted most of the 117 drones launched.

items.title: Swiss to vote on whether to cap population at 10 million
items.description: On June 14, Swiss voters will be able to decide on a far-right initiative to curb future immigration. What will it mean for the economy — and the country's European neighbors — if they approve the proposal?

items.title: Iran says draft U.S. deal includes oil sanctions waiver, nuclear limits and asset release
items.description: Iran and the U.S. draft a deal easing sanctions and addressing nuclear issues within 60 days.

items.title: Environment wins of the week
items.description: Good news about the state of the planet might seem to be in short supply but it's out there. Each week DW's environment desk brings you what went right.

items.title: Armed men kidnap high-ranking security official in Haiti
items.description: James Boyard's abduction is the highest-ranking abduction in the violence-wracked country in recent years, according to reports.

items.title: UK: Defense Secretary John Healey quits in military spending dispute
items.description: Britain's defense minister has abruptly quit his post, saying the government's plans to bolster defense spending in the coming years are inadequate. The move puts additional pressure on embattled UK PM Keir Starmer.

items.title: Trump’s name is gone from the Kennedy Center’s facade: top official
items.description: Workers begin removing Trump’s name from Kennedy Center after judge ruled it was illegally added to the building

items.title: Gaza Families search ruins after Israeli strikes hit homes
items.description: Palestinians in central Gaza searched through rubble on 12 June after Israeli strikes destroyed homes in Deir al-Balah and the Maghazi refugee camp, leaving dozens of displaced families without shelter and struggling to recover their belongings.

items.title: Trump says U.S. military strike killed leader of Tren de Aragua gang
items.description: President Trump said Friday that a U.S. strike has killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, whom he called "the infamous leader" of the Tren de Aragua gang in Venezuela.

items.title: Terrorists kill 20 in Kebbi attack
items.description: At least 20 people have been killed by suspected Lakurawa insurgents in a brutal attack on Sunday in Fasken Rafi, a village in the Arewa Local Government Area of Kebbi State. The post Terrorists kill 20 in Kebbi attack appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Argentina bonds jump, 2035s hit record high on S&P upgrade
items.description: S&P Global upgraded Argentina’s sovereign credit rating after markets closed Wednesday, citing positive fiscal results and other measures taken by Javier Milei’s administration. Leer más

items.title: Donald Trump turns 80 and celebrates with UFC cage fighting on the White House lawn
items.description: President Donald Trump turned 80 on Sunday and was set to celebrate with one of the more surreal spectacles in both sports and the nation's capital: cage fighting on the White House lawn. We discuss it with our international affairs commentator Douglas Herbert.

items.title: Broadcaster Enrique Macaya Márquez heads to his 18th World Cup at age 91
items.description: Veteran Argentine broadcaster will extend a record that stretches back to Sweden 1958, linking football's black-and-white era with the modern global game. Leer más

items.title: UK seizes suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker
items.description: UK forces on Sunday intercepted a sanctioned Russian ship, with navy commandos rappelling onboard from helicopters. The UK and Ukraine hailed the move, saying it robbed Russia of money for its ongoing war of aggression.

items.title: Manuel Neuer: The aura of a record-breaking goalkeeper
items.description: Germany's hopes at the 2026 World Cup are connected to world-class goalkeeper Manuel Neuer's fitness and form.

items.title: Lionel Messi plushies see roaring trade as China firms get World Cup 2026 boost
items.description: Sellers in China are betting on the country's lucrative fan market for a big World Cup boost. Leer más

items.title: All eyes on Messi – but don’t forget the supporting cast
items.description: Argentina can hurt opponents from anywhere on the pitch. There are plenty of candidates to become Messi’s latest World Cup partner-in-crime. Leer más

items.title: 2 Texas men charged after England team gear stolen en route to Kansas City for World Cup base
items.description: Prosecutors in Jackson County, Missouri, charged Mustafa Salik and Erfan Kamal, both of San Antonio, Texas, with the class D felony, which carries a sentence of up to seven years in prison; Bond was set at $75,000 for each of them

items.title: Woman dies in rope jump fall in Brazil
items.description: In videos of the accident circulating online, two men hoist the 21-year-old above their heads and launch her off a bridge in São Paulo.

items.title: PM’s Europe visit begins in Nice, with tech and bilateral ties in focus
items.description: In addition to Nice, this France visit includes programmes in Evian and Paris, with a possible bilateral meeting with U.S. President Trump
